import bpy
import bmesh
from mathutils import Vector, Euler

# Clear existing camera and target
if "Camera" in bpy.data.objects:
    bpy.data.objects.remove(bpy.data.objects["Camera"], do_unlink=True)
if "CameraTarget" in bpy.data.objects:
    bpy.data.objects.remove(bpy.data.objects["CameraTarget"], do_unlink=True)

# Create camera
bpy.ops.object.camera_add()
camera = bpy.context.object
camera.name = "Camera"

# Set camera properties
camera.location = (-3.9214415995223484, -3.4599026484749817, 1.5)
camera.rotation_euler = (-1.1825120555861899, 0, -1.8267579578284778)
camera.rotation_mode = 'XYZ'
camera.data.type = 'PERSP'
camera.data.lens_unit = 'FOV'
camera.data.lens = 60
camera.data.clip_start = 0.1
camera.data.clip_end = 450
camera.data.sensor_width = 32
camera.data.sensor_height = 18
camera.data.sensor_fit = 'AUTO'
camera.data.shift_x = 0
camera.data.shift_y = 0

# Create camera target (Empty object)
bpy.ops.object.empty_add(type='PLAIN_AXES')
target = bpy.context.object
target.name = "CameraTarget"
target.location = (-7.5841041028357425, 5.795697335609667, 0.5414738806718297)
target.rotation_euler = (0, 0, 0)
target.empty_display_size = 1

# Set camera to track target
camera.constraints.new(type='TRACK_TO')
camera.constraints["Track To"].target = target
camera.constraints["Track To"].track_axis = 'TRACK_NEGATIVE_Z'
camera.constraints["Track To"].up_axis = 'UP_Y'

# Set camera as active camera
bpy.context.scene.camera = camera

print("Camera setup complete!")
print(f"Camera location: {camera.location}")
print(f"Camera rotation: {camera.rotation_euler}")
print(f"Target location: {target.location}")
print(f"Camera FOV: {camera.data.lens}")
